---
—---
title: "Link between Experiences and Cognition"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r libraries}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = F, warning = F, message = F)

library(xgboost)
library(vip)
library(glmnet)
library(dplyr)
library(projpred)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(brms)
library(posterior)
library(bayesplot)
library(doRNG)
library(ggplotify)
library(ggfortify)
library(png)
library(grid)
library(latex2exp)
library(corrplot)
library(ggpubr)
library(broom)
library(cowplot)
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)
```

```{r load_data}
final_df <- read_csv("../data/final_feature_df.csv")

predictors <- c(
  "kcs_per_overall", "kcs_per_Interacting", "kcs_per_Not_Interacting",
  "total_speech_exposure_per_overall", "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting", "total_speech_exposure_per_Not_Interacting",
  "kcds_exposure_per_overall", "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting", "kcds_exposure_per_Not_Interacting",
  "ohs_exposure_per_overall", "ohs_exposure_per_Interacting", "ohs_exposure_per_Not_Interacting",
  "turns_per_minute_overall",
  "turns_per_minute_interacting",
  "agency_initiation_prop",
  "avg_turn_duration",
  "avg_block_duration",
  "avg_turns_per_block",
  "pct_face_visible", "pct_person_visible", "pct_face_or_person_visible", "avg_face_proximity",
  "pct_Interacting"
)

predictors <- c(
  "turns_per_minute_interacting", "agency_initiation_prop", "avg_turn_duration",
  "pct_face_or_person_visible",
  "pct_Not_Interacting",
  "ohs_exposure_per_Interacting",
  "kcs_per_overall", "kcs_per_Interacting",
  "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting", "kcds_exposure_per_Not_Interacting",
  "total_speech_exposure_per_Not_Interacting", "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting"
)
```

```{r postponed_projpred_orev}
# 1. Prepare Data
formula_string_orev <- paste("orev_successes | trials(orev_trials) ~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), "+ age_at_test")
final_orev_formula <- as.formula(formula_string_orev)

# 2. Define the search path
s_terms <- c("1",
             predictors, 
             paste0(paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), " + age_at_test"))

# Rescale data
proj_orev_full_data <- final_df %>% 
  select(orev_successes, orev_trials, age_at_test, child_id, all_of(predictors)) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  mutate(across(c(age_at_test, all_of(predictors)), as.numeric)) %>%
  mutate(across(c(age_at_test, all_of(predictors)), scale))

proj_orev_full_data$child_id <- as.factor(proj_orev_full_data$child_id)

# 2. Fit Reference Model
ref_orev_model <- brm(
  formula = final_orev_formula,
  data = proj_orev_full_data,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
  chains = 4, 
  iter = 4000, 
  warmup = 1000, 
  cores = 4, 
  seed = 123,
  refresh = 50,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.99, max_treedepth = 15),
  backend = "rstan",
  threads = threading(8),
)

# 3. Create Reference Object
ref_orev_obj <- get_refmodel(ref_orev_model)

# 2. Run final model
cvvs_orev_final <- cv_varsel(
  ref_orev_obj,
  method = 'forward',
  cv_method = 'loo',
  search_terms = s_terms,
  #nterms_max = length(s_terms),
  validate_search = TRUE,
  parallel = TRUE,
  threads = threading(8),
)

summary(cvvs_orev_final)
p_orev <- plot(cvvs_orev_final, stats = "mlpd", deltas = TRUE)

rk_orev <- ranking(cvvs_orev_final)
pr_rk_orev <- cv_proportions(rk_orev)
plot(pr_rk_orev)
```

```{r projpred_inference_orev}
# --- STEP D: POST-SELECTION INFERENCE ---
size_decided_orev <- 13
predictors_final_orev <- head(rk_orev[["fulldata"]], size_decided_orev) 
  
# 1. Project the reference model onto your chosen variables
prj_orev <- project(ref_orev_obj, 
                   predictor_terms = predictors_final_orev, 
                   ndraws = 4000)

# 2. Convert to a matrix so we can see the coefficients (Betas)
prj_mat_orev <- as.matrix(prj_orev)

# Convert to correct format for "posterior"
prj_drws_orev <- as_draws_matrix(prj_mat_orev)
# Calculate stats: Median, MAD (Standard Deviation equivalent), and 95% Intervals
prj_smmry_orev <- summarize_draws(
  prj_drws_orev,
  "median", "mad", function(x) quantile(x, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
  )
prj_smmry_orev <- as.data.frame(prj_smmry_orev)
print(prj_smmry_orev, digits = 1)
# save summary table
write.csv(prj_smmry_orev, "../outputs/prj_summary_orev.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# 3. Visualize the "Effect Size" for your top 4 variables
bayesplot_theme_set(ggplot2::theme_bw())
# Creates the visual "Forest Plot"
# If a variable's bar does not cross zero, it is a "significant" predictor.
# If it is to the right of zero, it increases OREV score.
# If it is to the left, it decreases it.
mcmc_intervals(prj_mat_orev) + 
  ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1.5, 1.6))

refm_mat_orev <- as.matrix(ref_orev_model)
mcmc_intervals(refm_mat_orev, pars = colnames(prj_mat_orev)) +
  ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1.5, 1.6))
```

```{r projpred_predictions_orev}
# 1. Generate simulated data
prj_predict_orev <- proj_predict(prj_orev)

# 2. Run the visual check
# Use ppc_bars for count data (integers 0-20)
ppc_bars(
  y = proj_orev_full_data$orev_successes,
  yrep = prj_predict_orev
) + 
  ggtitle("Orev Binomial Model Check: Real vs. Simulated Correct Items") +
  xlab("Number of Correct Items (out of 20)")
```

```{r postponed_projpred_prag}
# 1. Prepare Data
formula_string_prag <- paste("prag_successes | trials(prag_trials) ~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), "+ age_at_test")
final_prag_formula <- as.formula(formula_string_prag)

# Rescale data
proj_prag_full_data <- final_df %>% 
  select(prag_successes, prag_trials, age_at_test, child_id, all_of(predictors)) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  mutate(across(c(age_at_test, all_of(predictors)), as.numeric)) %>%
  mutate(across(c(age_at_test, all_of(predictors)), scale))

proj_prag_full_data$child_id <- as.factor(proj_prag_full_data$child_id)

# 2. Fit Reference Model
ref_prag_model <- brm(
  formula = final_prag_formula,
  data = proj_prag_full_data,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"), 
  #prior = set_prior(horseshoe(df = 1, par_ratio = par_ratio_orev)),
  chains = 4, 
  iter = 4000, 
  warmup = 1000, 
  cores = 4, 
  seed = 123,
  refresh = 50,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.99, max_treedepth = 15),
  backend = "rstan",
  threads = threading(8),
)

# 3. Create Reference Object
ref_prag_obj <- get_refmodel(ref_prag_model)

# 2. Run final model
cvvs_prag_final <- cv_varsel(
  ref_prag_obj,
  method = 'forward',
  cv_method = 'loo',
  search_terms = s_terms,
  validate_search = TRUE,
  parallel = TRUE,
  threads = threading(8),
)

summary(cvvs_prag_final)
p_prag <- plot(cvvs_prag_final, stats = "mlpd", deltas = TRUE)

rk_prag <- ranking(cvvs_prag_final)
pr_rk_prag <- cv_proportions(rk_prag)
plot(pr_rk_prag)

ggsave("../outputs/rk_prag.png", plot(pr_rk_prag), width = 20, height = 14, dpi = 300, scale= 0.5)

```

```{r projpred_inference_prag}
# --- STEP D: POST-SELECTION INFERENCE ---
size_decided_prag <- 13
predictors_final_prag <- head(rk_prag[["fulldata"]], size_decided_prag) 
  
# 1. Project the reference model onto your chosen variables
prj_prag <- project(ref_prag_obj, 
                   predictor_terms = predictors_final_prag, 
                   ndraws = 4000)

# 2. Convert to a matrix so we can see the coefficients (Betas)
prj_mat_prag <- as.matrix(prj_prag )

# Convert to correct format for "posterior"
prj_drws_prag  <- as_draws_matrix(prj_mat_prag)
# Calculate stats: Median, MAD (Standard Deviation equivalent), and 95% Intervals
prj_smmry_prag  <- summarize_draws(
  prj_drws_prag ,
  "median", "mad", function(x) quantile(x, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
  )
prj_smmry_prag <- as.data.frame(prj_smmry_prag)
print(prj_smmry_prag, digits = 1)
# save summary table
write.csv(prj_smmry_prag, "../outputs/prj_summary_prag.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# 3. Visualize the "Effect Size" for your top 4 variables
bayesplot_theme_set(ggplot2::theme_bw())
# Creates the visual "Forest Plot"
# If a variable's bar does not cross zero, it is a "significant" predictor.
# If it is to the right of zero, it increases OREV score.
# If it is to the left, it decreases it.
mcmc_intervals(prj_mat_prag) + 
  ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1.5, 1.6))

refm_mat_prag <- as.matrix(ref_prag_model)
mcmc_intervals(refm_mat_prag, pars = colnames(prj_mat_prag)) +
  ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1.5, 1.6))
```

```{r projpred_inference_prag}
# --- STEP D: POST-SELECTION INFERENCE ---

# 1. Project the reference model onto your 4 chosen variables
prj_prag <- project(ref_prag_obj, 
                   predictor_terms = predictors_final_prag, 
                   ndraws = 4000)

# 2. Convert to a matrix so we can see the coefficients (Betas)
prj_mat_prag <- as.matrix(prj_prag)

# Convert to correct format for "posterior"
prj_drws_prag <- as_draws_matrix(prj_mat_prag)
# Calculate stats: Median, MAD (Standard Deviation equivalent), and 95% Intervals
prj_smmry_prag <- summarize_draws(
  prj_drws_prag,
  "median", "mad", function(x) quantile(x, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
  )
prj_smmry_prag <- as.data.frame(prj_smmry_prag)
print(prj_smmry_prag, digits = 1)
# save summary table
write.csv(prj_smmry_prag, "../outputs/prj_summary_prag.csv", row.names = TRUE)

# 3. Visualize the "Effect Size" for your top variables
bayesplot_theme_set(ggplot2::theme_bw())
# Creates the visual "Forest Plot"
# If a variable's bar does not cross zero, it is a "significant" predictor.
# If it is to the right of zero, it increases OREV score.
# If it is to the left, it decreases it.
mcmc_intervals(prj_mat_prag) + 
  ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1.5, 1.6))

refm_mat_prag <- as.matrix(ref_prag_model)
mcmc_intervals(refm_mat_prag, pars = colnames(prj_mat_prag)) +
  ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1.5, 1.6))
```

```{r projpred_predictions_prag}
# 1. Generate simulated data
prj_predict_prag <- proj_predict(prj_prag)

# 2. Run visual check
# Use ppc_bars for count data (integers 0-20)
ppc_bars(
  y = proj_prag_full_data$prag_successes,
  yrep = prj_predict_prag
) + 
  ggtitle("Prag Binomial Model Check: Real vs. Simulated Correct Items") +
  xlab("Number of Correct Items (out of 20)")
```

```{r create_combined_plot}
label_dict <- list(
  # Demographics
  "age_at_test" = "age",

  # Global Ecology
  "pct_Interacting"     = "P[int]",
  "pct_Not_Interacting" = "P[paste('\u00AC', int)]",

  # Vocal Activity
  "kcs_per_overall"         = "KCS[all]",
  "kcs_per_Interacting"     = "KCS[int]",
  "kcs_per_Not_Interacting" = "KCS[paste('\u00AC',int)]",

  # Speech Exposure (total)
  "total_speech_exposure_per_overall"         = "Exps[all]",
  "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting"     = "Exps[int]",
  "total_speech_exposure_per_Not_Interacting" = "Exps[paste('\u00AC', int)]",

  # Child-directed speech
  "kcds_exposure_per_overall"         = "KCDS[all]",
  "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting"     = "KCDS[int]",
  "kcds_exposure_per_Not_Interacting" = "KCDS[paste('\u00AC', int)]",

  # Overheard speech
  "ohs_exposure_per_overall"         = "OHS[all]",
  "ohs_exposure_per_Interacting"     = "OHS[int]",
  "ohs_exposure_per_Not_Interacting" = "OHS[paste('\u00AC', int)]",

  # Turn-Taking
  "turns_per_minute_overall"     = "TpM[all]",
  "turns_per_minute_interacting" = "TpM[int]",
  "agency_initiation_prop"       = "P[agency]",
  "avg_turn_duration"            = "bar(D)[turn]",
  "avg_block_duration"           = "bar(D)[block]",
  # FIX: Using valid plotmath formatting for slash/fractions
  "avg_turns_per_block"          = "bar(N)[plain('turn/block')]",

  # Visuals
  "pct_face_visible"           = "P[face]",
  "pct_person_visible"         = "P[person]",
  # FIX: Using plain text grouping for combinations
  "pct_face_or_person_visible" = "P[plain('face/person')]",
  "avg_face_proximity"         = "bar(d)[face]"
)

paper_labels <- function(axis_breaks) {
  if (is.numeric(axis_breaks)) {
    indices <- as.integer(axis_breaks) + 1  
    raw_names <- existing_order_3[indices]
  } else {
    raw_names <- axis_breaks
  }
  
  new_labels <- sapply(raw_names, function(name) {
    if (is.na(name) || name == "") {
      return("''") # Return an explicit empty plotmath string literal
    } else if (name %in% names(label_dict)) {
      return(label_dict[[name]])
    } else {
      # Wrap completely in escaped quotes so parse never breaks on plain words
      return(paste0("plain('", name, "')"))
    }
  })
  
  # Crucial Fix: Vectorize the parsing safely so it retains full length
  return(as.expression(lapply(new_labels, function(x) parse(text = x)[[1]])))
}

p3 <- plot(cvvs_orev_final, stats = "mlpd", deltas = TRUE)
p4 <- plot(cvvs_prag_final, stats = "mlpd", deltas = TRUE)


# 2. Extract the existing order of the y-axis (Predictors)
existing_order_3 <- ggplot_build(p3)$layout$panel_params[[1]]$x$get_labels()
existing_order_4 <- ggplot_build(p4)$layout$panel_params[[1]]$x$get_labels()

title_style <- theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14))

master_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 24) + 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 40), # Very large titles
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 20),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
    #strip.background = element_blank(),
    #strip.text = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20)
  )

# 3. Apply your labels AND the limits to keep the order
increase_matrix_text <- function(p, new_size = 6) {
  p$layers[[which(sapply(p$layers, function(x) inherits(x$geom, "GeomText")))]]$aes_params$size <- new_size
  return(p)
}

p_orev_mlpd <- p3 + 
  master_theme +
  labs(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL) +
  labs(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, y = "Dif vs. \nref model") +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), strip.text = element_blank(), strip.background = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:(length(existing_order_3)-1), labels = paper_labels(existing_order_3))

p_prag_mlpd <- p4 + 
  master_theme +
  labs(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, y = "Dif vs. \nref model") +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20, margin = margin(t = 10)), strip.text = element_blank(), strip.background = element_blank()) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Submodel Size (Predictors added sequentially)", breaks = 0:(length(existing_order_4)-1), labels = paper_labels(existing_order_4))

# 3. Combine them vertically
combined_mlpd_plot_stacked <- plot_grid(
  p_orev_mlpd, 
  p_prag_mlpd,
  rel_heights = c(0.9, 1),
  align = "v",                          
  labels = c("C", "D"),
  label_fontface = "bold",
  ncol = 1
)

saveRDS(combined_mlpd_plot_stacked, "../outputs/mlpd_combined_plot_stacked.rds")
saveRDS(p_orev_mlpd, "../outputs/orev_mlpd.rds")
saveRDS(p_prag_mlpd, "../outputs/prag_mlpd.rds")

ggsave("../outputs/mlpd_combined_plot_stacked.png", combined_mlpd_plot_stacked, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 800) #scale= 0.85)
ggsave("../outputs/mlpd_combined_plot_stacked.png", combined_mlpd_plot_stacked, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 800) #scale= 0.85)
ggsave("../outputs/mlpd_combined_plot_stacked.png", combined_mlpd_plot_stacked, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 800) #scale= 0.85)
```

```{r mlpd_and_ranking_plots_side_by_side}
# Individual plots

p_orev_ranking_plot <- p1 + 
  master_theme +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = existing_order_1, labels = paper_labels) +
  title_style

p_prag_ranking_plot <- p2 + 
  master_theme +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = existing_order_2, labels = paper_labels) +
  title_style

shared_ylim <- c(-0.375, 0.0)

p_orev_mlpd_plot <- p_orev + 
  master_theme +              
  labs(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, y = "Dif vs. ref model", x = NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 0:(length(existing_order_3) - 1), 
    labels = paper_labels(existing_order_3)
  ) + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = shared_ylim) + 
  theme(                             
    plot.margin = margin(b = 10, r = 0) # Added bottom margin
  )

p_prag_mlpd_plot <- p_prag + 
  master_theme +            
  labs(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, y = NULL, x = NULL) + 
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 0:(length(existing_order_4) - 1), 
    labels = paper_labels(existing_order_4)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = shared_ylim) + 
  theme(                            
    axis.text.y  = element_blank(),     
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),     
    plot.margin  = margin(b = 10, l = 0) # Added bottom margin
  )

mlpd_plots_raw <- plot_grid(
  p_orev_mlpd_plot + theme(plot.margin = margin(r = 15, l = 5)), # 1. Added a slight right padding to oREV
  p_prag_mlpd_plot,
  nrow = 1,
  align = "h",                          
  rel_widths = c(1, 0.9),                                
  labels = c("A", "B"), 
  label_fontface = "bold",
  label_x = c(0.01, -0.015),  # Pulls 'B' out into the empty whitespace on the left
  hjust = 0                 # Keeps them neatly left-aligned at their new coordinates
)

clean_x_label <- textGrob(
  "Submodel Size (Predictors added sequentially by forward-ranking order)", 
  gp = gpar(fontsize = 24)
)

# Arrange the grid with the new bottom label
mlpd_plots <- grid.arrange(mlpd_plots_raw, bottom = clean_x_label)


ggsave("../outputs/predictor_ranking_orev.png", p_orev_ranking_plot, width = 20, height = 14, dpi = 300, scale= 0.5)
ggsave("../outputs/predictor_ranking_prag.png", p_prag_ranking_plot, width = 20, height = 14, dpi = 300, scale= 0.5)

ggsave("../outputs/mlpd_combined_plot_side_by_side.png", mlpd_plots, width = 20, height = 8, dpi = 600, scale = 0.80)
dggsave("../outputs/mlpd_orev.png", p_orev_mlpd_plot, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale= 0.80)
ggsave("../outputs/mlpd_prag.png", p_prag_mlpd_plot, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 300, scale= 0.80)
```
